rxn_class_summary: Visible-light photocatalytic remote double functionalization of 5-aryl-1,2,4-oxadiazolines to spiro-azalactams via energy-transfer-generated iminyl radicals and singlet oxygen.
electron_flow: fac-Ir(ppy)3 is photoexcited and undergoes triplet-triplet energy transfer in parallel to both the oxadiazoline 1a (giving 1a*) and to O2 (giving 1O2); 1O2 inserts at C5 of 1a* to form excited hydroperoxide D*, whose homolytic N–O cleavage releases HOO• and an iminyl radical A that performs ipso attack on the pendant arene, with the resulting spirocyclic radical E trapped by O2/HOO• at the para position to give 3a after loss of water.
rate_determining_step: Homolytic N–O bond cleavage of the excited hydroperoxy intermediate D* generating iminyl radical A (DFT TS1 N–O ≈ 24.9 kcal/mol on the favored pathway).

probe_reagents.name: 18O2 isotopic labeling
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a exclusively contained 18O, confirming O2 as the oxygen source incorporated at the para position.

probe_reagents.name: SmI2 (chemical one-electron reductant)
probe_reagents.observation: No 3a formed (0%); 1a recovered (94%), excluding a reductive SET pathway via radical anion B.

probe_reagents.name: Ceric ammonium nitrate (CAN)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave only 2a (20%) and trace 3a, ruling out oxidative SET pathway via radical cation C.

probe_reagents.name: Benzil (triplet sensitizer, ET = 2.57 eV)
probe_reagents.observation: Afforded 3a (32%), supporting triplet-triplet energy-transfer activation of 1a.

probe_reagents.name: 1,3-Diphenylisobenzofuran (DPBF)
probe_reagents.observation: Bleaching of DPBF absorbance confirmed 1O2 generation by fac-Ir(ppy)3; ΦΔ = 0.95.

probe_reagents.name: DABCO (1O2 quencher)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed (0% 3a), confirming requirement of singlet oxygen.

probe_reagents.name: NaN3 (1O2 quencher)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield reduced to 33%, consistent with 1O2 involvement.

probe_reagents.name: Sterically hindered 2,6-disubstituted substrate 1b
probe_reagents.observation: No 3b formed; ipso attack sterically disfavored, supporting iminyl radical ipso cyclization.

probe_reagents.name: Chemical 1O2 sources (NaOH/Na2MoO4/H2O2 or Ca(OH)2/H2O2)
probe_reagents.observation: No 3a formed (1a recovered 97%), showing 1a* (not ground-state 1a) is required.

probe_reagents.name: Benzoyl peroxide (PhCO2)2
probe_reagents.observation: Only 10% 3a with 31% 2a side product, demonstrating need for visible-light photocatalysis.

probe_reagents.name: Light on/off and photon flux variation
probe_reagents.observation: No dark reactivity; nonlinear photon-flux dependence consistent with two-photon excitation of both 1a and O2.

probe_reagents.name: Ferrioxalate actinometry
probe_reagents.observation: Quantum yield 16–18% (<50%), excluding radical chain propagation.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a / fac-Ir(ppy)3
probe_reagents.observation: Eox(1a)=+1.24 V, Ered(1a)=−2.16 V vs SCE vs E*red(Ir)=+0.16 V and E*ox(Ir)=−1.62 V — both SET directions thermodynamically forbidden.

notes: Authors favor dual energy-transfer (Dexter to 1a, confirmed by ln(ET rate) ∝ [1a]^(−1/3); Förster-like to O2) producing 1a* and 1O2 that combine to D*, then homolytic N–O cleavage to iminyl radical A and ipso arene attack. An alternative pathway (ortho-hydroperoxidation of spiro radical E followed by [1,3]-sigmatropic shift via intermediate F) is mentioned as plausible based on DFT spin densities (para 0.605 vs ortho 0.415). Electron-transfer pathways via radical cation C or radical anion B are explicitly excluded by redox potentials and by SmI2/CAN control experiments.
